description: Backend: UserProfile model, onboarding API, user_context utility, and daily briefing agent with tests
steps: 1. Update backend/app/db/models.py: Add UserProfile(BaseModel) class with fields role, occupation, work_hours_start (default 9), work_hours_end (default 18), wake_time (default 7), sleep_time (default 23), priorities (list[str]), daily_routine (str), goals (list[str]), onboarding_complete (bool=False). Add 'profile: UserProfile = Field(default_factory=UserProfile)' to the User model.
2. Create backend/app/api/ directory (does not exist yet). Create backend/app/api/__init__.py (empty).

3. Create backend/app/api/onboarding.py with a FastAPI APIRouter: POST /api/onboarding (accepts profile fields in body, requires auth_token as query param or header, saves to Firestore by updating user doc's profile field), GET /api/onboarding/status (returns {complete: bool} based on user profile.onboarding_complete), GET /api/profile (returns full profile dict). Use verify_google_token for auth. Use UserRepository to fetch/update user. If user not found, create one.
4. Register the onboarding router in backend/app/main.py using app.include_router(...).

5. Create backend/app/utils/user_context.py with async function get_user_context(user_id: str) -> str. Fetch user via UserRepository.get_by_id. If no profile or not onboarding_complete, return 'User has not completed onboarding.' Otherwise return formatted string with role, occupation, work hours, priorities, goals, daily routine.

6. Update backend/app/agents/orchestrator.py _analyze_intent method: import get_user_context, call it with user_id extracted from conversation_history context or task dict, inject the result alongside time_context_string() in the prompt. The user_id comes from the 'user' dict in task (task.get('user', {}).get('sub', '')).

7. Create backend/app/agents/briefing.py with async function generate_daily_briefing(user_id: str, auth_token: str, mcp_client) -> str. It fetches today's calendar events (via mcp_client.call_tool('google-calendar', 'list_events', ...)), pending tasks (via mcp_client.call_tool('google-tasks', 'list_tasks', ...)), user profile (via UserRepository). Uses Gemini (GenerativeModel) to compose a personalized morning briefing. Warm, concise, actionable. Handle errors gracefully.
8. Create backend/app/api/briefing.py with a FastAPI APIRouter: GET /api/briefing (requires auth_token, calls generate_daily_briefing, returns {briefing: str}). Register this router in main.py.

9. In orchestrator.py: detect when user says 'hello'/'hi'/'hey'/'good morning' AND it is a new day (check against a simple heuristic or just always provide briefing on greeting if profile exists), respond with the daily briefing instead of generic greeting. Modify the direct_response handling or add a check before routing.

10. Create backend/tests/test_onboarding.py: Test POST /api/onboarding (saves profile), GET /api/onboarding/status (returns correct boolean), GET /api/profile (returns profile data). Use app_client fixture and mock_firestore from conftest.
11. Create backend/tests/test_user_context.py: Test get_user_context returns correct string when profile exists, returns 'not completed' when no profile.
12. Create backend/tests/test_briefing.py: Test generate_daily_briefing with mocked MCP calls and Gemini. Verify it returns a string.
acceptance_criteria: python -m pytest tests/ -v passes all existing 75 tests plus new tests
POST /api/onboarding with valid token saves profile to Firestore mock
Orchestrator prompt now includes user context alongside time context
New test files (test_onboarding.py, test_user_context.py, test_briefing.py) all pass
findings: All 93 tests pass (75 original + 18 new). The briefing generator uses vertexai.generative_models.GenerativeModel accessed through the vertexai module namespace (not a direct from-import) so that conftest mock_vertexai_model fixture properly intercepts it. UserProfile model added to models.py with profile field on User. Onboarding API supports both query param auth_token and Authorization Bearer header. Orchestrator now injects user_context alongside time_context_string and generates briefing on greeting when user has completed onboarding.
